message:
With the warm weather settling in, here's a reminder from your vet that matters more than most people think: hydration and heat safety can be a genuine lifesaver for your dog this season. ☀️🐶

Dogs don't cool down the way we do. They can't sweat through their skin to shed heat — they rely mostly on panting, which is far less efficient, especially on hot, humid days. That makes fresh, clean water non-negotiable: keep it available at all times, bring some along on walks, and consider a second bowl outside. Learn the early warning signs of overheating, too — heavy panting, thick drool, bright red gums, wobbliness, or vomiting all mean it's time to cool your dog down and call your vet right away.

A few simple habits go a long way: walk during the cooler morning and evening hours, never leave your dog in a parked car even for a minute, and offer shade and water breaks during play. 💛

What's your favorite way to help your pup beat the heat? Share your tips below! 🐾
